summary: Before the first real user with live data, deletion is the default operation and ceremony is an operational bug — while governance gates stay fully active.

rules.rule: Treat DEV-MODE as a declared state: until the human authority explicitly declares the transition to production mode, assume the repo is in DEV-MODE.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: When finding contaminated, obsolete, or conceptually wrong code/data in DEV-MODE, delete it (files, tables, old APIs, seed data) rather than preserving it behind deprecation markers or data-preservation migrations — asking first only for a test datum that cost the human authority more than 1h to configure.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Do not ask the human authority to approve mechanical fixes, deletion of clearly obsolete code, mechanically-equivalent variants, sub-steps of an already-approved plan, or something already approved in a prior turn — one meta-level approval covers execution.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: DEV-MODE does not disable governance gates (security, forbidden vocabulary, schema/data lifecycle, auth guards, branch/worktree discipline, proposal preflight) — only the tempo changes; the safety gates are not the ceremony being cut.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Obtain human-authority approval even in DEV-MODE for operations that touch a sealed canon, the philosophy-root or meta-governance canon, monorepo architecture, a surface the human is manually tuning, or credentials/secrets (even test ones).

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate
